extraction.one_line_summary: SÁNCHEZ Luis León attacked solo with 12km remaining on the penultimate climb to win, ASGREEN Kasper took GC lead on minimal time gaps.
tactical_patterns: SÁNCHEZ Luis León timed his attack for 12km to go, exploiting the penultimate of six climbs when early break was absorbed.
ASGREEN Kasper gained GC lead through bonus second accumulation rather than time gaps, revealing stage control mattered more than pure climbing.
Teams allowed four-man early break including IMHOF Claudio who banked KOM points unopposed before controlled catch in final 35km.

rider_signals.SÁNCHEZ Luis León: positive: showed veteran timing by attacking exactly when early break caught with enough climbing left to hold off peloton
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: positive: took GC lead despite not winning, indicating Quick-Step positioned him perfectly for bonus seconds throughout stage
rider_signals.IMHOF Claudio: neutral: banked KOM points from breakaway but mechanical with 12km left coincided with peloton's planned catch timing
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: negative: lost GC lead by fractions despite starting as defending stage winner, suggesting Bahrain lacked control in finale
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: neutral: contested intermediate sprint for bonus seconds but couldn't follow late attack, showing GC focus over stage win
